title: Bogoliubov theory for trapped Bose-Einstein condensates
speaker: Benjamin Schlein
abstract: We consider systems of NN particles interacting through a repulsive potential in the Gross-Pitaevskii regime. We prove complete Bose-Einstein condensation and we determine the form of the low-energy spectrum, in the limit of large NN. Our results confirm the predictions of Bogoliubov theory. This talk is based on joint works with C. Boccato, C. Brennecke and S. Cenatiempo.

proof_stages.label: Gross-Pitaevskii many-body model and scattering length
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up N bosons in a unit periodic box with Hamiltonian containing the singular Gross-Pitaevskii interaction N^2V(Nx), explains the dilute scaling, and defines the scattering length a_0 and its scaling as a_0/N.

proof_stages.label: Known leading energy, condensation, and correlations
proof_stages.summary: Classical results identify the leading ground-state energy through the scattering length and prove Bose-Einstein condensation, while the factorized-state computation shows that condensate occupation alone misses the correlation energy.

proof_stages.label: Main translation-invariant Bogoliubov spectral theorems
proof_stages.summary: The new results sharpen condensation to the optimal N^{-1} depletion scale, compute the ground-state energy and depletion to order one, and describe the low-lying excitation spectrum through the Bogoliubov dispersion relation.

proof_stages.label: Comparison regimes, extensions, and limitations
proof_stages.summary: The speaker situates the theorem among mean-field and intermediate scalings, non-translation-invariant trapped systems, dynamical Gross-Pitaevskii limits, thermodynamic-limit Lee-Huang-Yang theory, and related open issues raised in questions.

proof_stages.label: Fock-space reformulation by second quantization
proof_stages.summary: To explain the proof heuristics, the N-particle Hamiltonian is rewritten on bosonic Fock space using creation and annihilation operators, so kinetic and interaction terms become quadratic and quartic operator expressions in momentum modes.

proof_stages.label: Bogoliubov c-number substitution and quadratic diagonalization
proof_stages.summary: Using condensation in the zero mode, Bogoliubov replaces zero-mode operators by sqrt(N), discards cubic and quartic fluctuation terms, and diagonalizes the resulting quadratic Hamiltonian by a Bogoliubov transformation to obtain a naive spectrum.

proof_stages.label: Renormalization from cubic and quartic fluctuation terms
proof_stages.summary: The rigorous argument must recover the replacement of the bare Fourier coefficient by the scattering length by extracting from the cubic and quartic terms exactly the correlation energy that the naive quadratic approximation omits.

examples.name: Quench by turning off the external field
examples.note: The physical protocol of preparing the trapped ground state and then switching off the trap motivates the many-body dynamics theorem leading to the time-dependent Gross-Pitaevskii equation.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker models the quench by removing the external field, evolving with the new Hamiltonian, and describing the resulting condensate dynamics via the time-dependent Gross-Pitaevskii equation
